title: Manulea dubia
taxonomy: Manulea dubia is classified within the family Scrophulariaceae. This family is a part of the order Lamiales in the angiosperm clade. The order Lamiales is further grouped under the subclass Asteridae in the class Magnoliopsida. The subclass Asteridae is a part of the larger group of flowering plants known as the eudicots. The class Magnoliopsida is a division within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Manulea dubia is known for its potential medicinal properties, with studies indicating its possible use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments. The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Additionally, Manulea dubia has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential applications. Further studies are necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
